article_meta.title: close Video American Accountability Foundation president releases Education Department 'watch list,' explains why Trump

reasoning: Mostly neutral paraphrase with attribution to the agency; minor inheritance of source framing via phrases like "so-called 'book bans'" and "restoring parents' rights," though both are presented as the department's characterization.

custom_reasoning.spin: Verbs are neutral (dismissed, eliminated, stated); slight spin in adopting "restoring" but attributed.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All evaluative claims ("meritless," "dubious legal theory") are clearly attributed to the agency.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Subjective framing appears as agency’s stance, not the model’s own; minor leakage with "restoring parents' rights."
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or alarming language beyond sourced quotes.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about motives beyond attributed statements.
custom_reasoning.slant: Emphasizes DOE perspective consistent with source; adds brief conservative context without over-weighting.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Leaves out specific Forsyth County/Title IX detail but no perspective present in source is omitted.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Uses "so-called" and "restoring," but both tied to the department’s framing.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Generally neutral; "so-called" reflects source language but not as the model’s assertion.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balanced tone matching the source content.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats official statements and political context evenly.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Noticeably more neutral than source, with minor inherited phrasing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Represents DOE and conservative context; AAF is listed though not quoted in summary, but present in the article headline.
